mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over a 6-month period signals rising inflation expectations, leading to higher interest rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or 5-day forward returns in high-inflation periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the unemployment rate over a 3-month period signals a tightening labor market, leading to higher wages and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) over a 6-month period signals rising economic activity, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 40% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or 60-day forward returns in high-growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the RSAFS (Retail Sales) over a 3-month period, combined with a 0.5% decrease in the unemployment rate over the same period, signals rising consumer spending and a tightening labor market, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising consumer spending and tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the federal funds rate over a 3-month period, combined with a 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the same period, signals a monetary tightening cycle, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or 5-day forward returns in monetary tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the payroll employment (PAYEMS) over a 3-month period, combined with a 1% increase in the average hourly earnings, signals rising labor costs and a tightening labor market, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising labor costs and tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% decrease in the housing starts (HOUST) over a 6-month period signals a decline in housing market activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 40% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or 60-day forward returns in declining housing market periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
